payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Source elements present in the ledger.

Support fulfillment of parental pocket money obligations

Visibility function allowing children to view their own completed tasks
Track children's claims on completed pocket money tasks
Enable parental fulfillment of earned pocket money obligations

payload.description: Task completion tracking process
payload.justification: Signal SG915 describes tracking task completion as core to enabling pocket money claims, indicating a strategic process.

payload.description: Compensable task definition process
payload.justification: Signal SG916 articulates parental need to define tasks with monetary values, establishing task definition as a strategic process.

payload.description: Task status visibility provisioning
payload.justification: Signal SG917 identifies need for visibility into task status, implying a process for providing that visibility to stakeholders.

payload.description: Weekly earnings calculation and presentation
payload.justification: Signal SG918 expresses purpose of providing weekly earnings totals, indicating a strategic process for calculating and presenting earnings.

payload.description: Pocket money obligation reporting
payload.justification: Signal SG919 articulates parental need for reporting to support obligation fulfillment, establishing reporting as a strategic process.

payload.description: Pocket money claim tracked by the enterprise
payload.justification: Signal SG915 describes enabling children to claim pocket money, indicating pocket money claim as a strategic entity tracked through task completion.

payload.description: Task with completion status
payload.justification: Signals SG915 and SG917 both reference tasks as core tracked items - completion tracking (SG915) and status visibility (SG917) indicate task as a strategic entity.

payload.description: Compensable task definition with monetary value
payload.justification: Signal SG916 identifies the need to define tasks with associated monetary values, establishing compensable task as a distinct strategic entity.

payload.description: Weekly earnings total
payload.justification: Signal SG918 expresses the purpose of providing weekly earnings totals, indicating this as a strategically important calculated entity.

payload.description: Historical record of pocket money activity
payload.justification: Signal SG920 indicates need for historical record retention, establishing historical records as a strategic entity for the enterprise.

payload.description: Weekly earnings calculation cycle
payload.justification: Signal SG918 references weekly earnings totals, indicating a weekly cycle that is strategically significant to the enterprise.

payload.description: Child stakeholder claiming pocket money
payload.justification: Signals SG915, SG917, and SG918 all reference children as primary stakeholders - claiming pocket money, needing task visibility, and receiving earnings information.

payload.description: Parent stakeholder managing compensable tasks
payload.justification: Signals SG916 and SG919 explicitly articulate parental needs - defining tasks and reporting on obligations - identifying parent as strategic actor.

payload.description: Enable children to claim earned pocket money and support fulfillment of parental pocket money obligations
payload.justification: Signal SG915 expresses core purpose of enabling pocket money claims, directly articulating a strategic goal of the enterprise. | Signal SG919 articulates parental need for reporting to support obligation fulfillment, indicating this support as a strategic goal.

payload.description: Provide transparency in child earnings
payload.justification: Signal SG918 expresses purpose of providing earnings totals for transparency, establishing transparency as a strategic goal.

payload.description: Historical record retention for organizational accountability
payload.justification: Signal SG920 indicates organizational need for historical record retention, suggesting a strategic principle of maintaining accountability through records.

payload.description: Task definition process enabling parents to create tasks with monetary values
payload.justification: Parental capability to define tasks represents a core business process in the system.

payload.description: Task claiming process where children assert completion of tasks
payload.justification: The core purpose involves children claiming completed tasks, representing a fundamental business process.

payload.description: Visibility function allowing children to view available tasks
payload.justification: Viewing available tasks is a distinct function within the child's interaction capability. | Viewing completed tasks is a separate visibility function distinct from viewing available tasks.

payload.description: Weekly aggregation function calculating total earnings per child
payload.justification: The capability to aggregate and display weekly earnings is a distinct calculation function.

payload.description: Parental reporting function linking children, tasks, and earnings for fulfillment
payload.justification: The reporting capability for parents to fulfill payment obligations is a distinct business function.

payload.description: Task availability resets at weekly temporal boundaries
payload.justification: The weekly reset of task availability is explicitly defined as a cyclical business rule.

payload.description: Historical task data must be retained and remain accessible
payload.justification: The requirement for retention and accessibility of historical data constitutes a business rule.

payload.description: Child as a business participant in the pocket money system
payload.justification: Multiple signals clearly establish children as core entities who complete tasks, view information, and have earnings tracked.

payload.description: Task as a completable activity with associated monetary value
payload.justification: Tasks are consistently referenced as core entities with monetary value, completion state, and availability status.

payload.description: Claim representing a child's assertion of task completion
payload.justification: The system's core purpose is tracking children's claims on completed tasks, establishing claims as a distinct entity.

payload.description: Monetary value associated with each task
payload.justification: Tasks have monetary value that aggregates into earnings, making this a fundamental attribute.

payload.description: Completion status indicating whether a task has been finished
payload.justification: The distinction between available and completed tasks indicates completion status as a key attribute.

payload.description: Weekly earnings total for each child
payload.justification: The weekly aggregation of monetary value earned by children is a distinct calculated attribute.

payload.description: Availability status determining which tasks can be claimed
payload.justification: Task availability is distinct from completion and resets weekly, indicating a separate state attribute.

payload.description: Task relationship between Parent and Child
payload.justification: Signal explicitly defines the fundamental relationship between child entities and completed task entities. | Establishes parental capability to define tasks, implying a parent-task creation relationship. | The linkage between children, tasks, and earnings establishes a derived relationship through task completion.

payload.description: Historical task data retained for access and reporting
payload.justification: Historical data is treated as a distinct conceptual entity requiring retention and accessibility.

payload.description: Weekly operational cycle governing task availability and earnings aggregation
payload.justification: Multiple signals establish a weekly cycle as the fundamental temporal pattern for task tracking and earnings.

payload.description: Weekly reset event that restores task availability
payload.justification: The weekly reset at temporal boundaries represents a recurring business event.

payload.description: Task completion event when a child finishes a task
payload.justification: The completion of tasks by children represents a significant business event that changes state.

payload.description: Child actor who completes tasks and tracks earnings
payload.justification: Children are consistently identified as primary actors who interact with tasks and view their earnings.

payload.description: Parent actor who defines tasks and reviews fulfillment reports
payload.justification: Parents are identified as actors with distinct capabilities for task definition and reporting access.

payload.description: Track children's claims on completed pocket money tasks and enable parental fulfillment of earned pocket money obligations
payload.justification: Signal explicitly defines this as the core conceptual purpose of the system. | The reporting capability serves the business goal of enabling parents to fulfill payment obligations.

payload.description: Children may only view tasks relevant to their own participation
payload.justification: The visibility requirement for children to see their own completed tasks implies a constraint on data access scope.

payload.description: Historical task data must remain accessible over time
payload.justification: The retention and accessibility requirement establishes a constraint on data lifecycle management.

payload.description: Task availability operates on a weekly cyclical basis
payload.justification: The weekly cycle represents a fundamental organizing principle for how the business operates.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL CellContentItem elements present in the ledger.

payload.name: Task Contribution Governance
payload.description: The enterprise's responsibility to define, monitor, and report on task-based contributions by stakeholders, ensuring clarity regarding expectations, completion status, and associated compensation criteria.

payload.name: Financial Transparency
payload.description: The enterprise's obligation to provide visibility into earnings, compensable activities, and financial obligations across stakeholder relationships, ensuring informed participation and accountability.

payload.name: Enterprise Record Stewardship
payload.description: The enterprise's commitment to retain and preserve historical evidence of financial activities, task completion, and obligation discharge for organizational accountability and auditability purposes.

payload.name: Stakeholder Participation Entitlement
payload.description: The enterprise's commitment to define and govern the relationship between parent and child stakeholders, establishing the basis for participation, roles, and the foundation upon which value exchange and obligations are built.

payload.name: Compensatory Value Definition
payload.description: The enterprise's accountability for establishing the monetary value framework governing task-based compensation, including the rules by which contribution translates into financial obligation and the parameters that define claim eligibility.

payload.name: Financial Claim Settlement
payload.description: The enterprise's obligation to recognize, track, and discharge monetary claims arising from stakeholder contributions, ensuring that accumulated obligations are fulfilled and formally closed through authorized payment mechanisms.

payload.name: Task Stewardship
payload.description: The business responsibility for establishing and governing task definitions, their monetary obligations, and the lifecycle rules that determine when tasks become available for children to undertake.

payload.name: Completion Accountability
payload.description: The business responsibility for capturing and validating children's assertions of task completion, establishing the basis for earned entitlements within the pocket money obligation framework.

payload.name: Earnings Settlement
payload.description: The business responsibility for calculating periodic earnings totals and providing parental oversight of accumulated obligations, enabling fulfillment of pocket money entitlements.

payload.name: Historical Record Stewardship
payload.description: The business responsibility for preserving historical task completion records and ensuring their ongoing accessibility to support accountability and retrospective reporting requirements.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Domain elements present in the ledger.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish and maintain task definitions that specify completion criteria and associated monetary compensation.
payload.rationale: The enterprise is accountable for defining the fundamental structure of compensable tasks, including what constitutes completion and the financial value attached to each task. This consolidates the entity definitions (task with completion status, compensable task with monetary value) and the process for establishing them.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain records of task completion status for all defined tasks.
payload.rationale: The enterprise commits to tracking and maintaining the current state of task completion across its scope. This reflects the task completion tracking process at enterprise commitment level.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide visibility of task status to stakeholders.
payload.rationale: The enterprise is accountable for ensuring stakeholders have access to task status information. This expresses the task status visibility provisioning process as an enterprise-level commitment.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise parent stakeholders as responsible parties for managing compensable task definitions.
payload.rationale: This establishes the structural relationship between parent stakeholders and compensable tasks within the enterprise's governance framework. The enterprise recognises this role and accountability assignment.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide transparency in child earnings.
payload.rationale: Why-column goal content expressing an enterprise governance commitment to transparency — a policy-level constraint on enterprise operations.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide visibility into task status for compensable activities.
payload.rationale: How-column process content expressing the enterprise capability to make task status visible to stakeholders — a functional obligation at enterprise scope.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish weekly earnings totals.
payload.rationale: What-column entity and How-column process content together express the enterprise obligation to determine and present aggregate weekly earnings — unified as a single commitment to establish the earnings total.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain historical records of pocket money activity.
payload.rationale: The Why-column principle establishes record retention for organizational accountability, while the What-column entity identifies the specific artifact (historical records of pocket money activity). Together they form a single enterprise obligation to maintain these records. 'Maintain' is appropriate Row 1 vocabulary expressing enterprise stewardship responsibility, avoiding system-level verbs like 'store' or 'retain'.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise child stakeholders and parent stakeholders as participants in the pocket money entitlement relationship.
payload.rationale: Establishes the two enterprise-recognised stakeholder classes that form the basis of the participation relationship. Both child and parent actors are structural entities at enterprise scope; their recognition is a precondition for any functional entitlement or obligation.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable children to claim earned pocket money.
payload.rationale: Derived from the Why-column goal. 'Enable' is an appropriate enterprise-scope verb expressing the commitment to provide the capability for children to claim entitlements. This is a functional commitment at the enterprise level, not a system-level implementation.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall support the fulfillment of parental pocket money obligations.
payload.rationale: Derived from the second clause of the Why-column goal. Expresses the enterprise's commitment to enable parents to meet their pocket money obligations. The goal contains two distinct enterprise commitments (enable children to claim; support parents to fulfill), warranting two Requirements.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain accountability for pocket money claims arising from compensable tasks.
payload.rationale: The enterprise must be accountable for the pocket money claim entity tracked within its scope; this is an enterprise-level governance commitment, not a system function.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish the definitions of compensable tasks.
payload.rationale: The enterprise performs the act of defining which tasks are eligible for compensation; this is a capability commitment at enterprise scope.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide weekly earnings calculations.
payload.rationale: The enterprise commits to determining weekly earnings values derived from completed compensable tasks.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall present weekly earnings to entitled parties.
payload.rationale: The earnings calculation process includes a distinct obligation to communicate or present the result; this is a separate enterprise commitment from the calculation itself.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise compensable tasks with associated monetary value.
payload.rationale: The enterprise must establish the foundational obligation to identify and acknowledge tasks that create a monetary claim against the enterprise.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain weekly earnings totals for stakeholders.
payload.rationale: Derived from the entity representing accumulated earnings and the weekly calculation cycle, establishing the enterprise's obligation to maintain aggregated claim values on a weekly basis.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide reporting of pocket money obligations.
payload.rationale: The reporting process represents an enterprise-level commitment to communicate accumulated monetary obligations to stakeholders.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of each task as a completable activity.
payload.rationale: Task entity existence is a core business artefact that must be recorded to support task stewardship responsibilities.

payload.statement: Each task shall be associated with a monetary value.
payload.rationale: Monetary value is a required attribute of the task entity, establishing the structural relationship between task and compensation.

payload.statement: Each task shall possess an availability status.
payload.rationale: Availability status is a required attribute of the task entity that governs which tasks are claimable by children.

payload.statement: The parent shall be responsible for defining tasks with monetary values.
payload.rationale: The task definition capability is a core business responsibility assigned to the parent role, enabling the establishment of compensated work opportunities.

payload.statement: The parent shall be responsible for reviewing fulfillment reports.
payload.rationale: The parent actor's accountability includes review of task completion evidence, a distinct business responsibility from task definition.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce task availability reset at weekly temporal boundaries.
payload.rationale: The weekly cyclical reset is a business rule governing task lifecycle behavior, constraining when tasks become available for claiming.

payload.statement: The business shall recognise the child as a participant in the pocket money system.
payload.rationale: Establishes the child as a recognised business role within the pocket money accountability framework.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of each child's claim of task completion.
payload.rationale: Captures the business responsibility to record completion assertions as the basis for earned entitlements.

payload.statement: Each claim shall associate the child with the asserted task completion.
payload.rationale: Defines the composition of a claim entity linking child participant to task assertion.

payload.statement: The business shall account for the completion status of each task.
payload.rationale: Establishes accountability for tracking whether tasks have been finished, triggered by completion events.

payload.statement: The business shall govern the task relationship between parent and child.
payload.rationale: Defines the structural association that underpins task assignment and completion accountability.

payload.statement: The business shall make available to each child a view of tasks relevant to their participation.
payload.rationale: Combines the visibility capability with the constraint that children see only their own relevant tasks.

payload.statement: The business shall enforce that children may only view tasks relevant to their own participation.
payload.rationale: Expresses the business rule constraining task visibility to maintain proper accountability boundaries.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a weekly earnings total for each child participant.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility to record aggregated earnings on a weekly cycle, supporting the business's accountability for pocket money obligations.

payload.statement: The business shall calculate weekly earnings totals per child based on completed task claims.
payload.rationale: Core business capability to aggregate earnings within the weekly operational cycle, derived from How-column function declaration.

payload.statement: The business shall make available to parents a report linking each child to their completed tasks and corresponding earnings.
payload.rationale: Business responsibility to provide parental oversight capability, enabling fulfillment of pocket money obligations.

payload.statement: The business shall be accountable for tracking children's claims on completed pocket money tasks.
payload.rationale: Business-level accountability obligation derived from the motivational goal, ensuring the business maintains records of earned entitlements.

payload.statement: Parents shall approve fulfillment of earned pocket money obligations.
payload.rationale: Business role responsibility assigning parental approval authority over earnings fulfillment, completing the settlement accountability chain.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of historical task completions.
payload.rationale: The What-column entity identifies historical task data as a business artefact; the How-column rule establishes the business obligation to retain it. This is a capability statement requiring the business to maintain these records.

payload.statement: The business shall ensure ongoing accessibility of historical task completion records.
payload.rationale: The Why-column constraint and How-column rule both establish accessibility as a governance requirement. This expresses a business rule constraining how historical records must be governed over time.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Requirement elements present in the ledger.

payload.title: High Level Description
payload.description: Section: High Level Description

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Segment elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Expresses the core purpose of enabling children to claim pocket money through task completion tracking.
Articulates the parental need to define compensable tasks with associated monetary values.
Identifies the child stakeholder need for visibility into available and completed task status.
Expresses the purpose of providing children with weekly earnings totals for transparency.
Articulates the parental need for reporting to support fulfillment of pocket money obligations.
Indicates organizational need for historical record retention without specifying implementation mechanism.
Introduces the conceptual scope of the system as a high-level overview section.
Defines the core conceptual purpose as tracking children's claims on completed pocket money tasks within a weekly cycle.
Establishes the conceptual entities of tasks with monetary value and the parental capability to define them.
Defines the fundamental relationship between child entities and completed task entities.
Specifies conceptual visibility requirements for children to view both available and their own completed tasks.
Defines a weekly aggregation capability concept for children to see their earned monetary total.
Establishes the parental reporting capability concept linking children, tasks, and earnings for fulfillment purposes.
Defines a weekly cyclical business rule where task availability resets at temporal boundaries.
Requires the conceptual retention and accessibility of historical task-related data entities.

This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Stakeholder elements present in the ledger.
